verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane matrix-facing component of complex V. However, it has well-documented ectopic surface expression: ecto-F1-ATPase is present on the plasma membrane of hepatocytes (HDL/apoA-I receptor), endothelial cells, and notably on tumor cells, where it has been pursued as a cancer target (anti-beta-F1 antibodies, IF1 ligands). Cancer/tissue-restricted ecto-display qualifies as contextual via cell_state_induced. Not yes because baseline localization is mitochondrial and surface display requires specific cellular contexts. Ruled out: lysosomal exocytosis (no); stable_surface_attachment (not covalent TM-tethered); dual_localization fits partially but the ecto-pool is most prominent in transformed/specialized states; tissue_restricted possible but cancer-state induction dominates.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Could equally be classified as dual_localization given constitutive hepatocyte/endothelial surface pool alongside cancer ecto-display.
